source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/01_HAD1717MC(MG)/▶ CE MDD/인허가 준비 자료(초기)/18. Appendix (ce 인증 문서 작성 icmc)/Appendix 3. Risk Management/A-RMR-01 Risk Management Report (en).docx

document.title: Risk Management Report
authorityOrJurisdiction: Enforcement Regulations of the Medical Device Act

sourceSummary.oneLine: Risk management report for HAD1717MG and HAD1717MC solid state X-ray imager products, based on EN ISO 14971:2012.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Used to identify, evaluate, control, and document risks for manufacturing safe products and supporting CE-mark-related documentation.
keyPoints: The report is controlled document A-RMR-0-01, issue date 2019.06.24, revision 0.
The technical construction file covers Solid state X-ray Imager products HAD1717MG and HAD1717MC requested to bear the CE mark.
Risk management methods and processes are stated to be based on EN ISO 14971:2012.
Initial identified hazards include electrical leakage/current hazards, incorrect measurement, erroneous data transfer, loss or deterioration of function, use error, and information hazards.
After risk control measures, all nine evaluated residual risks are reported as acceptable in the Green zone.

concepts.name: Risk management
concepts.evidence: Risk management: Systematic application of management policies, procedures and practices to the tasks of analyzing, evaluating, controlling and monitoring risk

concepts.name: Risk management file
concepts.evidence: Risk management file: Set of records and other documents that are produced by risk management

concepts.name: Risk analysis
concepts.evidence: Risk analysis: Systematic use of available information to identify hazards and to estimate the risk

concepts.name: Risk evaluation
concepts.evidence: Risk evaluation: Process of comparing the estimated risk against given risk criteria to determine the acceptability of the risk

concepts.name: Risk control
concepts.evidence: Risk control: Process in which decisions are made and measures implemented by which risks are reduced to, or maintained within, specified levels

concepts.name: Residual risk
concepts.evidence: Residual risk: Risk remaining after risk control measures has been taken

concepts.name: Production and post-production information
concepts.evidence: Information on post-production of this product is collected through various channels as below.

evidence.claim: The document is titled Risk Management Report.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk Management Report

evidence.claim: The document number is A-RMR-0-01.
evidence.evidenceText: Document No. A-RMR-0-01

evidence.claim: The issue date is 2019.06.24.
evidence.evidenceText: Issue Date 2019.06.24

evidence.claim: The revision number is 0.
evidence.evidenceText: Revision Date / Rev. No. 0

evidence.claim: The revision history identifies revision 0 dated 2019.06.24 as the first edition.
evidence.evidenceText: Revision No. 0 Rev. Date 2019.06.24 Revision Description First edition

evidence.claim: The report addresses potential risk factors in use of an X-ray detector manufactured by H&abyz Co.,Ltd.
evidence.evidenceText: This risk management report is created to investigate potential risk factors on the use of X-ray detector which H&abyz Co.,Ltd.(hereinafter referred as “the organization”) manufactures.

evidence.claim: The report is intended to determine risks, check requirements, identify other risk factors, and support manufacturing safe products.
evidence.evidenceText: The purposes for this report is to figure out the risks, check if they meet requirements of national and/or international requirements and figure out if there are any other risk factors existing so that potential risks are removed. Therefore, the organization is to use this report for the manufacturing of safe products.

evidence.claim: The selected risk management methods and processes are based on EN ISO 14971:2012.
evidence.evidenceText: The main methods and processes that are selected are based on the regulations of EN ISO 14971:2012.

evidence.claim: The listed legal requirements include Council Directive 93/42/EEC as amended by 2007/47/EC.
evidence.evidenceText: Council Directive 93/42/EEC as amended by 2007/47/EC of 5 September 2007 concerning medical devices.

evidence.claim: The listed legal requirements include the Medical Device Act and Enforcement Regulations of the Medical Device Act.
evidence.evidenceText: Medical Device Act, Enforcement Regulations of the Medical Device Act

evidence.claim: The listed standard requirements include EN ISO 13485:2012, EN ISO 14971:2012, ISO 10993 Series, EN ISO 15223-1:2016, EN 1041:2008, and MEDDEV guidance documents.
evidence.evidenceText: Standard requirements EN ISO 13485: 2012 ... EN ISO 14971: 2012 ... ISO 10993 Series ... EN ISO 15223-1:2016 ... EN 1041:2008 ... MEDDEV 2.7/1 rev.3 ... MEDDEV 2.12/1 rev.8 ... MEDDEV 2.12/2 rev.2

evidence.claim: The technical construction file relates to a Solid state X-ray Imager requested to bear the CE mark.
evidence.evidenceText: This technical construction file consists of documentation relating to product, Solid state X-ray Imager, which meet the requirements of the checklists and which are requested to bear the CE mark

evidence.claim: The product model names are HAD1717MG and HAD1717MC.
evidence.evidenceText: Trade Name (Model Name) HAD1717MG HAD1717MC

evidence.claim: The device is described as not sustaining or supporting life.
evidence.evidenceText: This medical device is not sustain or support life.

evidence.claim: The device is described as not implanted.
evidence.evidenceText: It is not the implanted medical device.

evidence.claim: The device is described as not supplied sterile and not intended to be sterilized.
evidence.evidenceText: This product is not sterilized.

evidence.claim: Maintenance is serviced by the manufacturer and calibration with a test instrument may be needed.
evidence.evidenceText: The manufacturer will service the maintenance of the equipment. There may be a need to calibrate the instrument with a test instrument that is required for maintenance or calibration.

evidence.claim: Safe-use information is provided to the end user through the user manual.
evidence.evidenceText: The instruction for safe usage is provided to end user through user manual.

evidence.claim: Risk evaluation criteria define risk level as severity multiplied by probability.
evidence.evidenceText: *Risk (LEVEL) = Severity x Probability

evidence.claim: The risk criteria define scores 1 to 6 as Green zone and scores 8 to 25 as Red zone.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk(LVEL) Zone 1 ~ 6 Green zone(Acc, Broadly Acceptable Zone) 8~ 25 Red zone(N/Acc, Not Acceptable Zoon)

evidence.claim: Identified hazards include line voltage, enclosure leakage current, earth leakage current, patient leakage current, incorrect measurement, erroneous data transfer, loss or deterioration of function, attention failure, rule-based failure, routine violation, incomplete instructions for use, inadequate description of performance characteristics, and inadequate specification of accessories.
evidence.evidenceText: R01 Line voltage ... R02 Enclosure leakage Current ... R03 Earth leakage Current ... R04 Patient leakage current ... R05 Incorrect measurement ... R06 Erroneous data transfer ... R07 Loss or deterioration of function ... R08 Attention failure ... R09 Rule-based failure ... R10 Routine Violation ... R11 Incomplete instructions for use ... R12 Inadequate description of performance characteristics ... R13 Inadequate specification of accessories to be used with the medical device

evidence.claim: Residual risk evaluation found hazards R05 through R13 acceptable after controls.
evidence.evidenceText: Residual risk evaluation list ... R05 Incorrect measurement ... Acceptable ... R13 Inadequate specification of accessories to be used with the medical device ... Acceptable

evidence.claim: After risk control, all nine evaluated items were controlled as Green zone and acceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: All of nine (9) items have been controlled as Green zone(Broadly Acceptable Zone) that risks are acceptable.

evidence.claim: The report states risk/benefit analysis was not related because all residual risks were accepted after risk control.
evidence.evidenceText: The organization has not related to Step 8(Risk Gain Analysis) since residual risks are all accepted after risk control on risk factors of 5 Item.

evidence.claim: The report states no new or different hazards were found after risk control measures.
evidence.evidenceText: New or different types of hazard on the organization’s products have not been found after risk control measure (Step 5 ~ Step 6).

evidence.claim: Overall residual risks are considered acceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Overall residual risks are minimized, therefore, it is considered to be acceptable.

evidence.claim: Post-production information is collected from customer satisfaction research, internal audit, internet product monitoring, similar-product information, legal requirements, clinical evaluation, government or published data, and press releases.
evidence.evidenceText: Information on post-production of this product is collected through various channels as below. Research for customer satisfaction (Survey) ... Internal audit ... Product monitoring through internet ... Information gathering on other companies’ similar products ... Information gathering and its application related to legal requirements based on MFDS or international requirements ... Clinical evaluation, MFDS government or thesis/data ... Press release such as newspapers and TV etc.

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata authoritySignal is FDA, but the document text supports CE MDD, European Community, Medical Device Act, and MFDS references rather than FDA.
Authority or jurisdiction is mixed: the text lists European CE/MDD legal requirements and Korean/MFDS-related legal requirements without clearly identifying a single competent authority.

Risk count evidence is internally inconsistent: the report lists hazards R01 through R13, says five hazards were derived, later says six Green zone and three Red zone items, and residual evaluation covers nine items.
OCR or source quality issues are present in terms such as Risk(LVEL), Not Acceptable Zoon, activites, and Risk Gain Analysis.
